title: Revolving and installment credit
description: Practice your understanding of revolving and installment credit

assessment_items.question_content:
Revolving credit means you can borrow money up to a certain amount. You can keep borrowing and paying back as long as you don't go over the amount. 

**What is this *amount* called?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Revolving credit means you can borrow money up to a certain amount. You can keep borrowing and paying back as long as you don't go over the amount. 

**What happens if you don't pay back the full amount?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: You will be charged a late fee.
Your credit limit will be reduced.
The entire balance must be paid the next month.

assessment_items.question_content:
Revolving credit means you can borrow money up to a certain amount. You can keep borrowing and paying back as long as you don't go over the amount. 

**Which of the following is an example of revolving credit?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Installment credit means you borrow a specific amount of money and pay it back in equal parts every month. 

**What is the amount of time you have to pay the money back called?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Installment credit means you borrow a specific amount of money and pay it back in equal parts every month. 

**What is the specific amount you borrow called?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Installment credit means you borrow a specific amount of money and pay it back in equal parts every month. 

**Which of the following is an example of installment credit?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

There are different types of credit, depending on how you borrow and repay the money. Two common types are revolving and installment credit.

**Which type has *a minimum payment*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

There are different types of credit, depending on how you borrow and repay the money. Two common types are revolving and installment credit.

**Which type has a *fixed* monthly payment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

There are different types of credit, depending on how you borrow and repay the money. Two common types are revolving and installment credit.

**In which type of credit can the balance only go *down*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

You are given two choices for paying for the car repair bill: loan A and loan B.

**Loan A:**

Terms: | 
 :-| :-
Amount borrowed | $\$1{,}000$
Annual percentage rate (APR) | $10\%$
Loan term | $12$ months
Monthly payment | $\$87.92$
Total interest paid | $\$54.99$
Total cost of the loan | $\$1{,}054.99$

**Loan B:**

 Terms: | 
:-| :-
Credit limit | $\$1{,}000$ 
Cash advance | $\$800$
Interest rate/ APR | $18\%$ (prime rate + $4.99\%$)
Minimum payment | $\$20$ or $4\%$ of the balance 
Late fee | $\$40$

**Which of these loans is *revolving* credit?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

You are given two choices for paying for the roof repair: loan X and loan Y.

**Loan X:**

Terms: | 
 :-| :-
Credit limit | $\$2{,}000$
Cash advance | $\$450$
Interest rate/ APR | $26\%$ (prime rate + $10.99\%$)
Minimum payment | $\$30$ or $3\%$ of the balance 
Late fee | $\$40$

**Loan Y:**

Terms: | 
 :-| :-
Amount borrowed | $\$2{,}000$
Annual percentage rate (APR) | $8\%$
Loan term | $18$ months
Monthly payment | $\$123.51$
Total interest paid | $\$222.18$
Total cost of the loan | $\$2{,}222.18$


**Which of these loans is *revolving* credit?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

You are given two choices for paying for the new furniture: loan A and loan B.

**Loan A:**

Terms: | 
 :-| :-
Amount borrowed | $\$500$
Annual percentage rate (APR) | $10\%$
Loan term | $12$ months
Monthly payment | $\$43.96$
Total interest paid | $\$27.49$
Total cost of the loan | $\$527.49$

**Loan B:**

 Terms: | 
 :-| :-
Credit limit | $\$500$ 
Cash advance | $\$400$
Interest rate/ APR | $22\%$ (prime rate + $8.99\%$)
Minimum payment | $\$10$ or $3\%$ of the balance 
Late fee | $\$35$

**Which of these loans is *revolving* credit?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

You recently applied for credit at your dentist's office, and you've been approved. Here are the terms of credit that you'll need to follow:

 Terms: | 
 :-| :-
Credit limit | $\$700$ 
Cash advance | $\$500$
Interest rate/ APR | $22\%$ (prime rate + $8.99\%$)
Minimum payment | $\$15$ or $3\%$ of the balance 
Late fee | $\$35$

**According to these terms, what is the *total amount* you're allowed to spend using this credit?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Your prescription eyeglasses cost more than you expected.  Your optometrist's office offers financing, and you've been approved. Here are the conditions of the credit that you'll need to follow:


 Conditions: | 
 :-| :-
Credit limit | $\$800$ 
Cash advance | $\$600$
Interest rate/ APR | $21\%$ (prime rate + $7.99\%$)
Minimum payment | $\$20$ or $4\%$ of the balance 
Late fee | $\$30$

**Based on these conditions, what is the *maximum amount* you're allowed to spend using this credit?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Your pet got sick and needs treatment.  You applied for a line of credit at your veterinarian's clinic, and you've been approved. Here are the conditions of the credit that you'll need to follow:

 Conditions: | 
 :-| :-
Credit limit | $\$1{,}000$ 
Cash advance | $\$500$
Interest rate/ APR | $18\%$ (prime rate + $6.99\%$)
Minimum payment | $\$25$ or $5\%$ of the balance 
Late fee | $\$35$

**Based on these conditions, what is the *maximum amount* you're allowed to spend using this credit?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

You recently applied for credit at your dentist's office, and you've been approved. Here are the terms of credit that you'll need to follow:

Terms: | 
 :-| :-
Principal | $\$1{,}000$
Interest rate | $10\%$
Repayment period | $12$ months
Monthly payment | $\$87.92$
Total interest paid | $\$54.99$
Total cost of the loan | $\$1{,}054.99$

**Given these terms, what is the *maximum amount* you can spend using this line of credit?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

You have recently been approved for a line of credit at your dental clinic. The following are the credit terms you have to follow:

Terms: | 
 :-| :-
Principal | $\$1{,}500$
Interest rate | $12\%$
Repayment period | $18$ months
Monthly payment | $\$95.64$
Total interest paid | $\$220.52$
Total cost of the loan | $\$1{,}720.52$

**Given these terms, what is the *maximum amount* you can spend using this line of credit?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

You were recently approved for a line of credit at your optometrist's office. The following are the credit terms you were given:

Terms: | 
 :-| :-
Principal | $\$2{,}000$
Interest rate | $10\%$
Repayment period | $24$ months
Monthly payment | $\$92.63$
Total interest paid | $\$222.12$
Total cost of the loan | $\$2{,}222.12$

**Given these terms, what is the *maximum amount* you can spend using this line of credit?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Both revolving and installment credits have certain advantages and disadvantages.

**What is a disadvantage of *revolving* credit over installment credit?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It can't be accessed again once paid off.
It can charge high interest and fees if not paid in full or on time.
The payment amount is set and cannot be changed.

assessment_items.question_content:
Both revolving and installment credits have certain advantages and disadvantages.

**What is an advantage of *installment* credit over revolving credit?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It can't be accessed again once paid off.
It can charge high interest and fees if not paid in full or on time.
The payment amount is set and predictable.

assessment_items.question_content:
Both revolving and installment credits have certain advantages and disadvantages.

**What is a disadvantage of *revolving* credit over installment credit?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It can create a cycle of debt.
It can't be accessed again once paid off.
The payment amount is set and predictable.
